preview_text:
The model favors Turkey at 38%, with the USA at 31% and a draw at 31%. Turkey holds a modest edge, but the tight clustering of probabilities indicates a competitive encounter where no outcome dominates.

The spread across the three outcomes is narrow, suggesting the match carries genuine uncertainty. A draw rates as plausible at nearly one-third, and the gap between a Turkey win and a USA win spans only 7 percentage points. The model expects close, competitive football with multiple realistic paths to the final whistle.

The market and model diverge noticeably on the USA and draw. Sportsbooks assign the USA 40%, roughly 9 percentage points higher than the model, while shortening the draw to 26%—a 5-point discount. Polymarket prices align more closely with the sportsbook view on the USA but remain even more pessimistic about a draw. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the gap warrants awareness that fundamentally different assessments of match structure underlie them.
